title: The Rise of Remote Work
passage: Remote work, also known as working from home, has become very popular. Many companies now allow their employees to work from their own homes instead of going to an office. This change offers several benefits. For employees, it means they can save time and money on commuting. They can also have more flexibility in their schedules, which can lead to a better work-life balance. For example, someone might be able to start work a little later or take a short break to deal with personal matters.  Companies can also benefit. They might save money on office space and utilities.  Some studies suggest that remote workers can be more productive because they have fewer distractions than in a busy office. However, remote work also has challenges. It can be difficult for some people to stay focused at home, especially if there are many distractions like family or household chores.  Maintaining a strong team connection can also be harder when people are not physically together.  Effective communication and clear expectations are very important for successful remote work.

vocabulary.definition: working from a location other than a central office, often from home.
the regular journey between one's home and place of work.
the ability to be easily changed or adapted.
the state of equilibrium in which a person equally prioritizes the demands of one's career and the demands of one's personal life.
services such as electricity, gas, and water.
things that prevent someone from giving their full attention to something.
routine tasks that need to be done in a home, such as cleaning and laundry.
a strong belief that something will happen or be the case.

questions.question: Remote work means employees always work from an office.
Saving time on commuting is a benefit for employees.
Companies always save money when employees work remotely.
Remote workers are always more productive than office workers.
It can be difficult to stay focused at home.
Which of the following is mentioned as a challenge of remote work?

ieltsTips: Read the questions carefully before you read the passage.
Underline keywords in the questions and look for them in the passage.
Pay attention to synonyms and paraphrased ideas.
For True/False/Not Given questions, if the statement is directly supported by the text, it's True. If it's contradicted, it's False. If there is no information about it, it's Not Given.
For Multiple Choice questions, read all the options before choosing the best answer based on the passage.
